# Temper AI — LLM Reference (llms.txt)

> For AI coding agents: Temper AI is a YAML-defined multi-agent workflow engine. Read this fully before creating, modifying, or debugging workflows.

---

## 1. Server

**Default port: 8420** (not 3000, not 8000, not 8080)

```
Dashboard:    http://127.0.0.1:8420/app/     (NOT the root "/")
API base:     http://127.0.0.1:8420/api/
API docs:     http://127.0.0.1:8420/api/docs
Health:       GET http://127.0.0.1:8420/api/health
WebSocket:    ws://127.0.0.1:8420/ws/{execution_id}
```

**Start:** `temper serve --port 8420` or `docker compose up`

DO NOT create a separate frontend server. The backend serves the React SPA at `/app/*`.
DO NOT run raw `uvicorn` — use `temper serve`.

**Providers are configured via environment variables:**

| Provider | Env var | Default model |
|----------|---------|---------------|
| openai | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | gpt-4o-mini |
| anthropic | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | claude-sonnet-4-20250514 |
| vllm | `VLLM_BASE_URL` | (set `VLLM_MODEL`) |
| ollama | `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` | llama3.2 |
| gemini | `GEMINI_API_KEY` | gemini-2.5-flash |

At least one provider must be configured or all workflows fail with "provider not configured".

---

## 2. Running Workflows

**Via API:**
```bash
POST http://127.0.0.1:8420/api/runs
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "workflow": "blog_writer",
  "inputs": {"task": "Write about LLMs"},
  "workspace_path": "/optional/path"
}
```
Returns `{"execution_id": "uuid", "status": "running"}`. Execution is async.

The `workflow` field is the `workflow.name` value from the YAML, not a file path.

**Via CLI:**
```bash
temper run blog_writer --input task="Write about LLMs" -v
temper run blog_writer --no-db       # ephemeral, no persistence
temper validate blog_writer          # validate without executing
```

**Other API endpoints:**
```
GET  /api/workflows/{execution_id}        # full execution with hierarchy
GET  /api/workflows?limit=20&status=running  # list runs
POST /api/runs/{id}/cancel
POST /api/runs/{id}/resume                # resume from checkpoint
POST /api/runs/fork                       # fork from checkpoint
POST /api/runs/{id}/approve/{node_name}   # approve a gate node
GET  /api/runs/{id}/gates                 # list waiting gates
GET  /api/runs/{id}/checkpoints
```

---

## 3. Workflow YAML

A workflow is a DAG of nodes. Nodes are either agent nodes (one agent) or stage nodes (multiple agents with a strategy).

```yaml
workflow:
  name: my_workflow                  # required, unique identifier
  description: "What this does"
  defaults:
    provider: openai                 # applies to all agents unless overridden
    model: gpt-4o-mini
  safety:
    policies:
      - type: budget
        max_cost_usd: 5.00
  nodes:
    - name: step_one
      type: agent
      agent: my_agent

    - name: step_two
      type: agent
      agent: another_agent
      depends_on: [step_one]
      input_map:
        result: step_one.output
```

Workflow files live in `configs/workflows/*.yaml`. Agent files in `configs/agents/*.yaml`.

**WRONG — provider/model on every node:**
```yaml
nodes:
  - name: step_one
    type: agent
    agent: my_agent
    provider: openai      # redundant
    model: gpt-4o-mini    # redundant
  - name: step_two
    type: agent
    agent: my_agent
    provider: openai      # redundant
    model: gpt-4o-mini    # redundant
```

**RIGHT — set once in defaults, override only when needed:**
```yaml
workflow:
  defaults:
    provider: openai
    model: gpt-4o-mini
  nodes:
    - name: step_one
      type: agent
      agent: my_agent            # inherits defaults
    - name: expensive_step
      type: agent
      agent: deep_analyzer
      model: gpt-4o              # override only here
```

---

## 4. Node Types: `agent` vs `stage`

| Use `type: agent` | Use `type: stage` |
|---|---|
| Single agent doing one task | Multiple agents with a shared strategy |
| Sequential pipeline steps | Parallel review, leader synthesis, sequential chain |

**WRONG — parallel agents as separate top-level nodes:**
```yaml
# WRONG — no shared context, no synthesis, must manually wire with input_map
nodes:
  - name: analyst
    type: agent
    agent: code_analyst
  - name: security
    type: agent
    agent: security_reviewer
  - name: synthesizer
    type: agent
    agent: reviewer
    depends_on: [analyst, security]
    input_map:
      analyst_out: analyst.output
      security_out: security.output
```

**RIGHT — use a stage with a strategy:**
```yaml
# RIGHT — workers run in parallel, leader auto-receives all outputs
nodes:
  - name: review
    type: stage
    strategy: leader
    agents:
      - code_analyst
      - security_reviewer
      - agent: reviewer
        role: leader
        task_template: |
          Findings: {{ other_agents }}
          Original: {{ task }}
          Synthesize into a verdict.
  - name: improve
    type: agent
    agent: code_improver
    depends_on: [review]
    input_map:
      review: review.output    # leader's output
```

---

## 5. Strategies

Three built-in strategies (only valid on `type: stage` nodes):

### `parallel`
All agents run concurrently with no dependencies between them. Min 2 agents.
```yaml
- name: implement
  type: stage
  strategy: parallel
  agents: [frontend_dev, backend_dev]
```

### `sequential`
Linear chain. Each agent receives the previous agent's output in `{{ other_agents }}`. Min 2 agents.
```yaml
- name: pipeline
  type: stage
  strategy: sequential
  agents: [researcher, drafter, editor]
```

### `leader`
Workers run in parallel, then one leader synthesizes. The leader receives `{{ other_agents }}` with all worker outputs. Mark the leader with `role: leader` — if omitted, the last agent is the leader. Min 2 agents.
```yaml
- name: review
  type: stage
  strategy: leader
  agents:
    - code_analyst              # worker
    - security_reviewer         # worker
    - agent: reviewer           # leader
      role: leader
```

**Stage agents support inline overrides:**
```yaml
agents:
  - frontend_dev                         # shorthand, uses agent config as-is
  - agent: backend_dev                   # dict form with overrides
    task_template: "Custom: {{ task }}"
    role: leader
```

---

## 6. input_map — Wiring Data Between Nodes

`input_map` maps Jinja2 variable names in the receiving agent's template to source references from other nodes.

**Source reference formats:**

| Format | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `node_name.output` | Full text output of a node |
| `node_name.structured.field` | Field from JSON-structured output |
| `node_name.structured.a.b` | Nested field traversal |
| `node_name.status` | Status string of a node |
| `workflow.field` or `input.field` | Workflow-level input |
| `stage_name.child_node.output` | Child node inside a stage |

**WRONG:**
```yaml
input_map:
  data: step_one                    # missing .output
  verdict: review.verdict           # missing .structured.
  result: step_one.result           # .result doesn't exist, use .output
```

**RIGHT:**
```yaml
input_map:
  data: step_one.output
  verdict: review.structured.verdict
  task: workflow.task
```

The mapped keys become Jinja2 variables in the agent's `task_template`:
```yaml
task_template: |
  Data: {{ data }}
  Verdict: {{ verdict }}
```

**Without input_map:** the node receives all workflow inputs plus `{{ other_agents }}` (formatted outputs of all `depends_on` nodes).

---

## 7. Agent YAML

### LLM Agent

```yaml
agent:
  name: code_analyst
  type: llm
  system_prompt: |
    You are a code quality analyst.
  task_template: |
    Review this code: {{ code }}
    Output JSON: {"verdict": "PASS", "issues": [...]}
  tools:
    - Bash
    - FileWriter
  max_iterations: 10       # tool-calling loop limit (default 10)
  token_budget: 8000       # max input tokens (default 8000)
  total_timeout: 300       # seconds (default 300)
```

Do NOT set `provider` or `model` in agent YAML unless it genuinely must differ from the workflow default.

### Script Agent

Runs a bash script, no LLM. The last JSON-parseable line of stdout becomes `structured_output`.

```yaml
agent:
  name: build_checker
  type: script
  script_template: |
    #!/bin/bash
    set -e
    cd {{ workspace_path }}
    npm run build 2>&1
    echo '{"success": true}'
  timeout_seconds: 60      # default 30
```

Input variables from `input_data` are available as Jinja2 variables (string values are shell-quoted).

### Structured Output

Agents that output JSON can declare fields for compile-time validation and downstream field-level wiring:

```yaml
# On the node in the workflow:
- name: analyze
  type: agent
  agent: planner
  outputs:
    verdict: string        # "complex" or "simple"
    components: list
    approach: string
```

Types: `string`, `list`, `dict`, `number`, `boolean`, `any`

Then reference downstream: `analyze.structured.verdict`, `analyze.structured.components`

---

## 8. Tools

Reference by exact name (case-sensitive):

| Name | Description |
|---|---|
| `Bash` | Shell commands (sanitized env, command allowlist) |
| `FileWriter` | Write files with path protection |
| `FileEdit` | Exact-string replacement in files |
| `FileAppend` | Append to files |
| `Delegate` | Spawn sub-agents as visible DAG nodes |
| `WebSearch` | Web search via SearXNG |
| `Calculator` | Safe math evaluation |
| `git` | Git operations in workspace |
| `http` | HTTP requests |

**WRONG — wrong case:**
```yaml
tools:
  - bash          # WRONG, must be "Bash"
  - file_writer   # WRONG, must be "FileWriter"
```

MCP tools are configured in `configs/mcp_servers/*.yaml` and referenced by `<server>.<tool>` in agent `tools` lists.

---

## 9. Conditions and Loops

**Conditional execution** — skip a node unless condition is met:
```yaml
- name: fix
  type: agent
  agent: fixer
  depends_on: [review]
  condition:
    source: review.structured.verdict
    operator: equals
    value: "NEEDS_WORK"
```

Operators: `equals`, `not_equals`, `contains`, `in`, `exists`, `not_exists`

**Loops** — rewind to an earlier node:
```yaml
- name: check
  type: agent
  agent: checker
  depends_on: [fix]
  loop_to: fix             # rewind target
  max_loops: 3             # safety limit (default 1)
  loop_condition:
    source: check.structured.verdict
    operator: equals
    value: "NEEDS_WORK"
```

Both `loop_to` and `loop_condition` are required together. The agent must output valid JSON for structured field references.

---

## 10. Gates (Human-in-the-Loop)

```yaml
- name: deploy
  type: agent
  agent: deployer
  gate: true               # pauses, waits for approval
  depends_on: [review]
```

Approve: `POST /api/runs/{execution_id}/approve/deploy`

---

## 11. Safety Policies

Declared under `workflow.safety.policies`. Evaluated before every tool call. First-deny-wins.

```yaml
safety:
  policies:
    - type: budget
      max_cost_usd: 5.00
      max_tokens: 500000

    - type: file_access
      allowed_paths: ["/workspace"]
      denied_paths: [".env", "credentials", "/etc/"]

    - type: forbidden_ops
      # uses safe defaults: rm -rf /, DROP TABLE, curl | sh, etc.
```

Skip a policy per node: `skip_policies: ["budget"]`

---

## 12. Workflow Outputs

Map named outputs for programmatic consumption:

```yaml
workflow:
  name: my_workflow
  outputs:
    final_result: edit.output
    verdict: review.structured.verdict
  nodes: [...]
```

---

## 13. Env Var Substitution

Anywhere in YAML values:
- `${VAR}` — substituted at read time; error if unset
- `${VAR:default}` — uses `default` if `VAR` is unset

Works in system_prompt, task_template, script_template, MCP server args, etc.

---

## 14. Config File Locations

```
configs/
  workflows/       workflow YAML files
  agents/          agent YAML files
  stages/          reusable stage configs
  mcp_servers/     MCP server configs
  tools/           custom tool configs
```

The server auto-loads all YAML from `configs/` on startup. Override: `--config-dir /path`.

---

## 15. Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---|---|
| Accessing dashboard at `/` or creating a separate server | Use `http://127.0.0.1:8420/app/` |
| Running raw `uvicorn` instead of `temper serve` | Use `temper serve --port 8420` |
| Parallel agents as separate top-level nodes | Use `type: stage` + `strategy: parallel` or `leader` |
| `node_name` alone in input_map | Use `node_name.output` or `node_name.structured.field` |
| `node_name.result` or `node_name.json.field` | Use `node_name.output` or `node_name.structured.field` |
| Setting provider/model on every agent | Set once in `workflow.defaults` |
| Wrong tool casing (`bash`, `file_writer`) | Exact names: `Bash`, `FileWriter`, `FileEdit`, `WebSearch` |
| `strategy` on a `type: agent` node | `strategy` and `agents` only work on `type: stage` |
| Both `agents` and `nodes` on a stage | Mutually exclusive — pick one |
| `loop_condition` without `loop_to` | Both are required together |
| Referencing workflow by file path in API | Use the `workflow.name` value from YAML |
| Forgetting `depends_on` for sequential flow | Nodes without `depends_on` run immediately |

---

## 16. Minimal Working Example

```yaml
# configs/workflows/my_pipeline.yaml
workflow:
  name: my_pipeline
  defaults:
    provider: openai
    model: gpt-4o-mini
  safety:
    policies:
      - type: budget
        max_cost_usd: 2.00
  nodes:
    - name: research
      type: agent
      agent: topic_researcher

    - name: review
      type: stage
      strategy: leader
      depends_on: [research]
      input_map:
        research: research.output
      agents:
        - code_analyst
        - security_reviewer
        - agent: reviewer
          role: leader
          task_template: |
            Research: {{ research }}
            Findings: {{ other_agents }}
            Produce a final verdict.

    - name: improve
      type: agent
      agent: code_improver
      depends_on: [review]
      condition:
        source: review.structured.verdict
        operator: equals
        value: "FAIL"
      input_map:
        review: review.output
```

```bash
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8420/api/runs \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"workflow": "my_pipeline", "inputs": {"task": "LLM caching strategies"}}'
```
